title: WSL podman fails to connect if ~/.ssh does not exist
body:
### Issue Description

On WSL, if the user does not have a `.ssh` dir, we get a failure when connecting to a podman machine.  It is probably the same for a remote connection? And it might be only WSL, but I would guess not.



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. On a fresh WSL install with podman 5.3.1, run `podman machine init --now`
2. Run any podman command like `podman run -it --rm alpine ls`
3.


### Describe the results you received
Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
PS C:\Users\baude> podman run -it alpine ls
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: open C:\Users\baude\.ssh\known_hosts: The system cannot find the path specified.

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

### podman info output

Note: The Podman client is 5.3.1 but downloads a 5.3.0 machine image because it is WSL
